task_completion.description: Agent opens the 'Add Data' dialog or navigates to the file location in a file explorer for drag-and-drop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Add Data' window is visible, or a system file explorer window is open showing the 'SampleData' folder.

task_completion.description: Agent selects the specific file 'MRHead.nrrd' for loading.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The file 'MRHead.nrrd' is highlighted/selected in the file browser or the 'Add Data' file list.

task_completion.description: The 3D Slicer slice views transition from empty (black) to displaying image data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Red, Yellow, and Green slice view windows in the Slicer interface are no longer blank; they show grayscale image content.

task_completion.description: The loaded data is visually confirmed to be a brain MRI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The slice views clearly show anatomical structures of a human head, such as the skull, brain tissue, and ventricles.

task_completion.description: The loaded volume is identified as 'MRHead' within the Slicer interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'MRHead' is visible in the Data module list, the Subject Hierarchy, or the corner annotations of the slice views.

integrity.description: Agent is using the 3D Slicer application, not a generic image viewer or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The application window contains the 3D Slicer logo, module panel, and standard 3D Slicer toolbar layout.

integrity.description: Agent used the GUI (File menu or Drag-and-Drop) as requested, rather than purely scripting in the Python console.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the 'File' menu, 'Add Data' button, or dragging a file, rather than just typing code in the Python Interactor.

integrity.description: The result is a live rendering within the software, not a static screenshot opened in an image viewer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Slicer UI controls (sliders, buttons) are visible around the image data, indicating an active session.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is 'MRHead.nrrd'. It contains a volumetric MRI brain scan. When loaded into 3D Slicer, the slice views (Axial, Sagittal, Coronal) should display grayscale cross-sections of a human head/brain. The volume should be listed as 'MRHead' in the Data module or view annotations.
